reviewer_notes: Core FFmpeg DNN operand-index mechanism is right, but semantic hunk coverage is incomplete: anchors cover dnn_backend_native.c top-level operand hunk only and miss layer-loader operand-index guard hunks.
Rejected by function binding: CodeAnchor names ReadXPMImage without function_id. Patch hunk and model semantics are plausible, but structural gate rejects anchor, predicates, and fix set.
curl UAF mechanism is correct, but evidence link is incomplete because anchors omit multi.c lifecycle hunks that explain how lastconnect becomes stale after handle or connection removal.

httpd mod_proxy_uwsgi core mechanism is supported across both fix commits: pktsize type widening plus APR_UINT16_MAX guard. Caveat: model conflates 16K wording with actual 65535 guard and overstates potential buffer overflow.

Raw response is fenced JSON, but schema validation fails because code_anchors.1.path through code_anchors.4.path are missing. Raw KVM SEV cache-flush semantics are plausible, but parse/schema failure forces overall 0.
Linux bonding root cause is exact: bond_ipsec_add_sa dereferenced bond->curr_active_slave via slave->dev without a NULL check; patch adds if (!slave) unlock and return -ENODEV.

openjpeg mechanism is supported: image, l_stream, l_codec, and cstr_index NULL initialization moved into the per-image loop. Direct evidence supports double-free; UAF wording is unsupported overreach.
OpenSSL tls1_check_sig_alg NULL dereference is supported by sigalg != NULL guard. Caveat: model generalizes NULL source and omits TLS 1.3 SSL_check_chain callback trigger constraints.

QEMU ati_2d_blt direction is partly right, but model treats all patch changes as root cause. bpp and stride checks are supported; division-by-zero, broad OOB, integer overflow/underflow, and coordinate-update trigger are overbroad.

Wireshark NFS recursion-depth mechanism is correct. Caveat: patch is depth-threshold based, not visited-set cycle detection; anchors include include/expert registration plumbing beyond minimal root-cause evidence.
